action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 13 eval achieved a new best mean_r2=0.5259 with the 79M-param ResMLP (2560 hidden, 6 blocks, dropout 0.1) trained for 15 epochs. The main weakness remains ptend_q (0.3244). We have ~2.0 hours and 17 iterations left. Analysis: Training a larger model from scratch is risky and time-consuming. A quicker, safer way to boost score is to ensemble existing strong checkpoints (best.pt at 0.5259 and resmlp.pt at 0.5206). Averaging predictions reduces variance and often improves R2. We can also attempt to include large.pt if it loads. Decision: Update predict.py to load all available checkpoints, average their predictions, zero low-variance columns, and submit evaluation immediately. This costs no training time.
action.command: mkdir -p /tmp/test_data && python -c "import numpy as np; np.save('/tmp/test_data/scoring_input.npy', np.random.randn(100,124).astype('float32'))" && python predict.py --data_path /tmp/test_data --checkpoint checkpoints/best.pt --output /tmp/test_out.json && echo 'Predict OK'
